FAQ.title: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.subtitle: Everything you need to know about ReelKey
FAQ.contact: Still have questions? Contact us at

general.question: What is ReelKey?
general.answer: ReelKey is a BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) AI video generation workspace. You connect your own fal.ai API key, generate through supported models, and pay fal.ai directly for provider usage.

commercial.question: How is this different from other AI video platforms?
commercial.answer: ReelKey separates the workspace from provider billing. ReelKey handles the interface, generation history, and storage features; fal.ai bills generation costs directly to your own account.

aiModels.question: Which AI models are supported?
aiModels.answer: ReelKey currently supports Kling 2.5 Turbo Pro and Wan 2.5 via fal.ai — covering text-to-video and image-to-video modes at multiple resolutions. Additional models are added regularly, and all future additions are included in the lifetime license at no extra charge.

providerBilling.question: Who bills generation costs?
providerBilling.answer: fal.ai bills generation costs to your own fal.ai account. ReelKey does not resell provider usage in BYOK mode.

refund.question: What's your refund policy?
refund.answer: We offer a 14-day money-back guarantee. If ReelKey doesn't work with your API keys or you're not satisfied, contact support@reelkey.app within 14 days for a full refund. No questions asked.

support.question: Are my API keys safe?
support.answer: Yes. Your fal.ai API key is stored only in your browser's localStorage and is never transmitted to or stored on our servers. When you generate a video, your browser sends the key directly in the request; our server uses it in-memory for that single request only and immediately discards it.

Footer.tagline: Transform your ideas into stunning videos with AI.

resetPassword.body: We received a request to reset your password for your ReelKey account.

ApiUsage.title: API usage
ApiUsage.description: Track generation requests made with your fal.ai key. Estimated cost only includes completed requests.
ApiUsage.historyDescription: Cost is counted only after a request completes. Failed requests are shown for debugging and are not included in estimated cost.
